checks.a_analysis: The output includes a section titled '[Pages 1-13 unavailable]' which contains status messages explaining the conversion failure for those pages. While this is not found in the original text, it is considered an acceptable system-generated label for accessibility and navigation. No other fabricated body text or invented paragraphs were identified in the provided sections.

All contact information including phone numbers (855-662-2911, 202-418-0530, etc.), email addresses (911NMAC.Admin@NC.GOV, fcc504@fcc.gov, etc.), and physical addresses were cross-referenced against the source PDF and confirmed to be accurate.

The conversion results are missing approximately the first 12.5 pages of the source document. Specifically, the Agenda (p. 1), Minutes (pp. 2-5), FCC NPRM Background (pp. 6-7), FCC Fact Sheet (p. 8), and the first seven paragraphs of the FCC NPRM (pp. 9-12) are entirely absent from the output. This represents a critical loss of content (roughly 27% of the document).
checks.b_evidence_source: Content from pages 1 through the middle of page 13.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON starts at index 0 with text from the end of paragraph 8 on page 13: 'to Congress on 911 fees]."' and contains a placeholder section at index 20 indicating 'Pages 1-13 unavailable'.

checks.a_analysis: Multiple tables in the financial/contractual sections are significantly truncated. In Section 18 (KOMO Contract), the output only captures the first line item (N 1) while omitting items N 2, N 3, and N 4. In Section 19 (KXLY Invoice), the output begins at Line 3, omitting Line 1 and Line 2 entirely. This loss of data is greater than 20% for these specific tables.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 43 (KOMO): Items N 2, N 3, N 4. Page 45 (KXLY): Items 1 and 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 18 only shows 'N 1'. Section 19 begins text with 'Line 3'.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While there is a massive loss of whole pages (addressed in C3), the sections that were successfully processed (House Bills, Next Gen info, NMAC, WA Jobs) appear to be largely complete based on the text segments provided.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor text differences were evaluated. The output accurately preserves complex formatting such as strikethroughs and underlines in legislative text (e.g., HB 203), which demonstrates high fidelity in the text that was converted.

checks.a_analysis: I scanned the output JSON for any significant text blocks or sections that appear more than once. The content flows from the middle of the FCC document through the state legislation and onto the broadcast documentation without redundant repetitions of paragraphs or sections. While there is a summary of missing content at the end, it does not duplicate text already present.

The heading hierarchy is largely preserved or flattened in an acceptable manner. Major document titles (e.g., 'APPENDIX A', 'HOUSE BILL 203', 'WA 911 Jobs') are designated as section titles or H2 level headings. Sub-sections within these documents (like 'Budget Breakdown' or 'Definitions') are consistently assigned H2 or H3 levels that follow a logical nesting pattern relative to the section titles. No critical skips (e.g., H1 to H4) were observed.

A major structural failure occurs in Section 18 ('CONTRACT'). The line item table from PDF page 43, which contains 13 distinct columns (Line, Ch, Start Date, End Date, etc.), is collapsed into a 2-column structure in the JSON table 'KOMO spots and rates'. This merging combines unrelated data fields into single cells (e.g., 'N 1 KOMO10/12/20 11/'), rendering the tabular data unintelligible.
checks.b_evidence_source: The line item table on PDF page 43 contains 13 columns: *Line, Ch, Start Date, End Date, Description, Start/End Time, Days, Length, Spots/Week, Rate, Type, Spots, Amount.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON table 'KOMO spots and rates' has only 2 columns. Header 1: 'N 1 KOMO10/12/20 11/'. Header 2: '3 $4,500.00'.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is materially compromised. PDF pages 1 through 12, which include the meeting agenda, minutes, and the first four pages of the main FCC document, are missing from the start of the output. These pages are moved to the very end of the JSON as a placeholder section (Section 20) with an error message. The main content of the JSON starts mid-sentence on PDF page 12/13, disrupting the intended logical flow of the combined documents.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document should begin with the 'Education Committee Meeting Agenda' on PDF page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON content begins with Section 1 at the end of paragraph 8 of the FCC document (PDF page 13). PDF pages 1-12 are relegated to Section 20 at the end.

checks.a_analysis: Lists are generally converted correctly. The ordered list in FCC Section B (page 17) is correctly captured as an ordered list in JSON Section 4. Unordered bulleted lists in the 'WA 911 Jobs' section (page 40) and the NMAC section (page 39) are correctly represented as unordered lists in the JSON. The presence of line numbers in the legislation sections being merged into paragraphs is an acceptable formatting choice for this document type.

The links in the output were verified against the source text. Email addresses (e.g., 911NMAC.Admin@NC.GOV) and various government URLs (mil.wa.gov, fcc.gov) are present, clickable, and point to the correct destinations as found in the PDF. No fabricated or broken links were detected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly mirrors the visual organization of the source PDF. The document is a complex compilation of agenda items, minutes, legal notices, bills, and reports. The HTML maintains this logical grouping through clear section headings and a generated table of contents for navigation. Tables and text blocks are placed in the correct sequence relative to the source content. While the styling is adapted for web display, the information architecture remains faithful to the original.

All tables throughout the document are correctly rendered. This includes the attendance and voting tables in the minutes (pages 2-5), the complex multi-row/column contract details (page 43), and the itemized invoice tables (pages 45-46). Columns are aligned, headers are visually distinct, and the data remains fully legible and understandable in the HTML layout.

Several meaningful images are missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo found in the header of the source (pages 1-2) is absent. More critically, the two large, complex diagrams on source page 38—'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' and 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture'—are completely missing. The headings for these diagrams appear in the HTML, but there is no content or image rendered beneath them. These diagrams provide essential technical information that is lost in the conversion.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF on page 38 contains two meaningful diagrams: 'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' and 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture'. Page 1 also contains the NC DIT logo.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the headings for these sections ('NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' and 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture'), but no content or images are rendered below them. The agency logo is also missing from the document top.

checks.a_analysis: I have carefully scanned the rendered HTML output and did not find any instances of duplicated body content. Each paragraph, table, and legal section appears exactly once in the sequence corresponding to the source PDF. The presence of headings in both the navigation TOC and the body is an expected accessibility feature and not content duplication.
